title: Quadrance Product PASS: every QA area element is irreducibly a two-factor product
_fixture_purpose: Proves that every QA area-type element is a product of two role-distinct base elements. Quadrances (A=a*a, B=b*b) are products, not powers. S1 (b*b not b**2) encodes this structurally. Even 1*1=1 is an area, not a scalar. The 'square as special rectangle' collapses role-distinction into value-equality — an observer projection.
source_attribution: Will Dale, 2026-04-08. Insight: a square is NOT a 'special case' of rectangle where sides are equal. It is a rectangle whose observer projection hides the role-distinction between its two factors. 1*1 = 1^2 is still an AREA, not the integer 1. Parallels circle impossibility [207]. S1 axiom (b*b not b**2) is the structural encoding of this principle.

product_table.description: Every QA area element expressed as a product of two base-level elements (b, e, d, a). The two factors always have distinct structural roles even when their values coincide.

area_elements.note: S1 mandates a*a, not a**2. The product form preserves two-factor structure.
Fundamental quadrance. b*b is area, not power.

factor_1.role: first derived coordinate (b + e mod m)

area_elements.note: d = b+e, so D = (b+e)*(b+e). Still a product of two things.
Green quadrance (Qg). Two distinct base elements, never collapses.
Also F = d*d - e*e = (d-e)(d+e). Either form is a product of two distinct things.
From [183] Eisenstein Crystal: Z - Y = J = bd. Always a product.
X = ed = C/2. Half the green quadrance.
L = abde/6 = CF/12. Product of two area elements. Always integer for primitive directions.
G = D + E. Not itself a simple product but a SUM of two products (d*d + e*e). Blue quadrance (Qb). F*F + C*C = G*G.

role_distinction_proof.theorem: In every QA product element, the two factors have distinct structural roles even when their values are numerically equal. A quadrance b*b is NOT the same as 'b squared' — it is the product of b-as-base-coordinate and b-as-multiplier.
role_distinction_proof.proof_by_structure: The QA tuple (b, e, d, a) assigns each coordinate a distinct role: b = base state, e = generator, d = b+e (first derived), a = b+2e (second derived). When computing A = a*a, the first 'a' is the fourth coordinate of the tuple; the second 'a' is a scaling factor. Their values coincide but their roles in the algebraic structure are distinct. This is why S1 mandates b*b (product, two operands) over b**2 (power, one operand): the product form preserves role-distinction.
role_distinction_proof.s1_deep_reason: S1 (No **2) is not merely a floating-point guard. It is the structural encoding of irreducible two-factor products. b**2 collapses two roles into one; b*b preserves them. The power operator destroys dimensional information.

square_rectangle_parallel.standard_view: A square is a 'special case' of rectangle where length equals width. The rectangle 'loses' a degree of freedom, collapsing to a simpler shape.
square_rectangle_parallel.qa_view: A square is a rectangle whose two side-roles happen to have equal values. The product structure (length * width) is IRREDUCIBLE — it never collapses to a single factor. Even when length = width = 1, the area 1*1 = 1 is still a PRODUCT of two things, not the integer 1.

key_example.standard_interpretation: 1 squared = 1; the product collapses to a scalar
key_example.qa_interpretation: 1 * 1 = area of 1; the product of two unit lengths. Numerically identical to the length 1, but dimensionally distinct. The coincidence 1^2 = 1 is unique to the unit — for all other values, n^2 != n.

circle_parallel.circle_207: Observer sees equal axes → concludes C=0. But C=2de >= 2 always. The eccentricity is hidden along the viewing axis.
circle_parallel.square_208: Observer sees equal sides → concludes 'special case, one parameter.' But the product has TWO factors with distinct roles. The role-distinction is hidden by value-equality.

B_min.note: B = b*b = 1*1 = 1. The unit area. Still a product.

C_min.note: C = 2*d*e = 2*2*1 = 4 where d = b+e = 2 (raw, A2). Certified in [207].

exhaustive_area_minimum.note: d = b+e > e always (b >= 1), so F = b*a = d*d - e*e > 0 always. No degenerate cases. All products well-defined positive integers.

witnesses.note: The minimal b,e state. B=1 and E=1 are unit areas (1*1). d=1+1=2, a=1+2=3 (raw A2). Even unit products have two factors.
Equal-value state. B=E=9 (equal quadrances because b=e=3). d=3+3=6, a=3+6=9. Equal values, distinct roles: b is base, e is generator.
d=2+1=3, a=2+2=4. The (2,1) STATE has d=3. All products irreducible.
d=5+2=7, a=5+4=9. Every product has two distinct-role factors.

cross_references.name: QA Circle Impossibility
cross_references.relation: Parallel impossibility: circle (C=0) impossible, square non-collapse (product irreducible). Both are observer projections of 'special cases' that QA does not permit.

cross_references.name: QA Sixteen Identities
cross_references.relation: The 16 identities relate area elements. Every identity preserves the product structure — no identity collapses a product to a scalar.

cross_references.name: QA Eisenstein Norm
cross_references.relation: F=ab (product form) enters F^2-FW+W^2=Z^2. The Eisenstein norm is built from products of products.

cross_references.name: QA Koenig Twisted Squares
cross_references.relation: H=C+F, I=C-F. Even sums/differences of area elements remain area-type. H^2-G^2=2CF is a product of products.

cross_references.name: QA Eisenstein Crystal
cross_references.relation: J=bd certified. Two-factor product structure is load-bearing for the crystal constant encoder.

cross_references.name: QA Chromogeometry
cross_references.relation: F=Qr, C=Qg, G=Qb. All three chromogeometric quadrances are products or sums-of-products. The color classification operates on area-type objects.
